expectations.text: Grounds the deletion right in Art. 18 (item V — anonymization, blocking or deletion of unnecessary/excessive/noncompliant data; item VI — deletion of data processed based on consent) rather than inventing rights
expectations.evidence: Response only cites 'Art. 18, VI' for consent-based deletion ('the right to deletion/elimination of personal data (Art. 18, VI)'). It never mentions item V (or any item) for the separate right to anonymization/blocking/deletion of unnecessary, excessive, or non-compliant data — that half of the required grounding is entirely absent.

expectations.text: Includes identity verification of the requester before acting on the request
expectations.evidence: "Verify the requester's identity before acting. LGPD does not prescribe a specific method, but you must take reasonable steps to confirm the person emailing is actually the data subject."

expectations.text: Covers deletion across all three named systems (CRM, email platform, analytics tool) plus backups, and notifying processors/sub-processors so the deletion is replicated
expectations.evidence: Section 3 explicitly covers CRM, Email platform, Analytics tool, a dedicated 'Backups' bullet ('you should ensure deleted data is not restored and is purged according to your normal backup rotation/retention schedule'), and 'Third-party processors (operadores)... you as the controller are responsible for instructing them to delete the data too.'

expectations.text: States lawful retention exceptions under Art. 16 (compliance with legal/regulatory obligation such as tax and transaction records, research with anonymization where possible, transfer per legal requirements, or exclusive non-shared controller use) and advises telling the user what was retained and why
expectations.evidence: Response cites only Art. 16, I (legal/regulatory obligation) correctly. It mislabels Art. 16, II as 'regular exercise of rights in judicial, administrative, or arbitration proceedings' rather than the correct 'research with anonymization' exception, and never mentions the transfer-per-legal-requirement or exclusive/non-shared controller-use exceptions at all. Three of the four required exceptions are missing or misstated.

expectations.text: Gives a response timeframe consistent with LGPD/ANPD practice (15 days is the Art. 19 reference for the full access report; deletion 'without undue delay') and does NOT invent a statutory 24/48/72-hour deletion deadline; advises logging the request and outcome for accountability
expectations.evidence: "LGPD does not set a hard statutory deadline for responding to rights requests (unlike GDPR's 30 days)... many organizations align with a ~15-day target as a matter of good practice." No invented 24/48/72-hour deadline. Section 6 'Document the Process' advises maintaining a record of the request, verification, actions taken, and response for accountability (Art. 6, X).
